# Bleed

Allows content to bleed outside its parent's padding, creating edge-to-edge layouts within padded containers.

## Import

```jsx
import { Bleed } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| inline | `string \| number` | - | Bleed on both inline (left/right) sides |
| block | `string \| number` | - | Bleed on both block (top/bottom) sides |
| inlineStart | `string \| number` | - | Bleed on the inline start (left in LTR) |
| inlineEnd | `string \| number` | - | Bleed on the inline end (right in LTR) |
| blockStart | `string \| number` | - | Bleed on the block start (top) |
| blockEnd | `string \| number` | - | Bleed on the block end (bottom) |

## Usage

```jsx
<Box padding="10" borderWidth="1px">
  <Bleed inline="10">
    <Box bg="primary.100" height="20">
      Full-width content inside padded container
    </Box>
  </Bleed>
</Box>
```

## Features

- Extends content beyond parent padding
- Supports logical properties (inline/block)
- Directional control for fine-grained layouts
